source.purpose: Live convenience validation of Node 24 migration rules, not a prevalence estimate
source.selection: Two public repositories with current Node 20 migration issues plus one recently remediated repository

demandEvidence.limitations: GitHub code search counts are point-in-time directional signals, can include forks or inactive files, and do not prove adoption or willingness to pay. Two queries were not counted before the search rate limit was reached.

representativeFindings.title: actions/checkout@v4 declares Node 20
representativeFindings.fix: Upgrade to actions/checkout@v5 and test the workflow on Node 24.

representativeFindings.title: actions/upload-artifact@v4 declares Node 20
representativeFindings.fix: Upgrade to actions/upload-artifact@v6 and test the workflow on Node 24.

representativeFindings.title: actions/checkout@v4 declares Node 20
representativeFindings.fix: Upgrade to actions/checkout@v5 and test the workflow on Node 24.

representativeFindings.title: actions/setup-python@v5 declares Node 20
representativeFindings.fix: Upgrade to actions/setup-python@v6 and test the workflow on Node 24.
